description: Protein folding - prediction, dynamics, misfolding diseases.

facts.context: AlphaFold2 solved structure prediction but not:
distractors: static three-dimensional structure determination
sequence to structure relationship mapping
evolutionary conservation pattern analysis

facts.context: Levinthal's paradox highlights that proteins fold fast despite:
distractors: extremely restricted number of different amino acid residue types that are available
completely identical polypeptide sequences being conserved across all biological species
straightforward two-state folding transitions without intermediate conformational states

facts.context: Intrinsically disordered proteins challenge folding paradigms because:
distractors: they fold faster than ordered proteins do
they have no biological function at all
they always aggregate into toxic amyloid
directly specifying the final folded structure
synthesizing proteins from amino acids
degrading all misfolded proteins immediately

facts.context: The folding funnel model describes folding as:
facts.truth: energy landscape guiding to native state minimum
distractors: random walk with no energetic preference
single pathway with defined intermediates
uphill energy climb to functional state
protein underproduction and deficiency
correct folding but wrong localization
only genetic mutations not misfolding

facts.truth: inducing misfolding of normal protein copies
distractors: viral replication with nucleic acid genome
bacterial cell division and spreading
genetic mutation in host DNA sequence

facts.truth: while still being synthesized on ribosome
distractors: only after complete release from ribosome
exclusively in the endoplasmic reticulum
without any ribosome involvement at all

facts.context: Multi-domain protein folding is complex because:
facts.truth: domains can misfold or aggregate during synthesis
distractors: each domain folds independently without issues
multi-domain proteins do not exist in nature
only single-domain proteins can misfold
membrane proteins fold identically to soluble ones
membrane proteins have no tertiary structure
folding occurs before membrane insertion

facts.context: De novo protein design validates folding understanding by:
facts.truth: creating new folds not found in nature
distractors: only copying existing natural protein folds
proving that folding cannot be predicted
showing all possible folds already exist

facts.truth: local energy minima that slow reaching native state
distractors: the same as the native state minimum
beneficial for achieving correct fold faster
only theoretical and never observed experimentally
